preview_text:
The model favors England at 63%, with Panama assigned an 12% chance of victory. The underdog's path to three points is narrow but not negligible given the neutral venue and group-stage context, where individual matches carry weight.

The 25% draw probability signals a moderately competitive encounter. England's advantage is substantial but not overwhelming; a stalemate ranks as the second-likeliest outcome, reflecting meaningful uncertainty around match flow and execution. Panama enters as clear outsider, yet the distribution suggests this is not a heavily one-sided fixture in probabilistic terms.

The market diverges from the model in material fashion. Sportsbooks and Polymarket both assign England a win probability around 74–76%, roughly 11 percentage points higher than the model's 63%. Both sources converge tightly on Panama (9.6–9.9%) and draw rates near 15–16%, substantially below the model's 25%. The dashboard does not resolve which assessment better reflects true match likelihood; the gap warrants attention from users comparing forecasting approaches.
